name: This is the second fundamental principle of God's law of retribution. Every man will be
introduction:
rewarded for the services he has rendered, none will be rewarded for services rendered by
others. The same applies to punishment. It is the one who is guilty who will be punished. It
is possible, however, that if a man has initiated either good or bad practices, they will
continue to affect people's lives. The resulting good and bad deeds of people will be
reckoned either to their credit or against them, since they are clearly related to their efforts
and actions. It is impossible, however, that a map should be either rewarded for an act of
goodness or punished for an act of evil in which he has had no share - neither by intent nor
practical action. The requital of acts is not transferable.
